zh.lifeSummary: 當John Blake Pope II於1580年8月4日出生在英國武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特時，他的父親Sir John Pope24歲，母親Patience Alice Blakie27歲。他於1610年之前與Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth在英國英格蘭布里斯托結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1649年6月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特享年68歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Blake Pope II于1580年8月4日出生在英国武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特时，他的父亲Sir John Pope24岁，母亲Patience Alice Blakie27岁。他于1610年之前与Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth在英国英格兰布里斯托结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1649年6月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特享年68岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。

nameMeanings.name: Pope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English pope (derived via Old English from Late Latin papa ‘bishop, pope’, from Greek pappas ‘father’, in origin a nursery word.) In the early Christian Church, the Latin term was at first used as a title of respect for male clergy of every rank, but in the Western Church it gradually came to be restricted to bishops, and then only to the bishop of Rome; in the Eastern Church it continued to be used of all priests (see Popov , Papas ). The nickname would have been used for a vain or pompous man, or for someone who had played the part of the pope in a pageant or play. The surname is also present in Ireland and Scotland.

variants.name: Popov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Nikolay, Oleg, Grigoriy, Anatoly, Andrei, Arkadi, Dmitriy, Mikhail, Sergey.  Bulgarian or Macedonian Atanas, Dimitar, Yordan.  Serbian Bosko, Bozidar, Branko.

Russian, Bulgarian, Macedonian, Serbian, and Rusyn: patronymic from pop ‘priest’, from Greek pappas ‘father’ (compare English Pope ). The name may sometimes derive from a nickname, but celibacy was not enjoined on priests of the Orthodox Church and so the name can indeed mean ‘son of the priest’. In many cases, however, the Russian name means no more than ‘of the priest’ and designates a servant of a priest. Compare Popoff , see also Pop 2, Popovic , and Popovski 1.
Jewish (American): most likely a shortened form of   Popovski   2. Compare   Popoff  .

variants.name: Papke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Otto, Willi.
North German (also Päpke): nickname from a diminutive of Low German pape, Dutch paepe ‘pope’. Compare Pope .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Blake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which has two quite distinct etymologies. It is both from Old English blæc ‘black’ and from Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ it was thus originally a nickname given to someone with hair or skin that was either remarkably dark or remarkably light. It is now quite popular as a boy's name.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When John Blake Pope II was born on 4 August 1580, in Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England, his father, Sir John Pope, was 24 and his mother, Patience Alice Blakie, was 27. He married Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth before 1610, in Bristol, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 1 June 1649, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 68, and was buried in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Blake Pope II est né le 4 août 1580, à Kidderminster, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Pope, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Patience Alice Blakie, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth avant 1610, à Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 1 juin 1649, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Blake Pope II è nato il il 4 agosto 1580 nel Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England, il suo padre, Sir John Pope, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Patience Alice Blakie, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth prima del 1610, nel Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 1 giugno 1649, nel Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: John Blake Pope IIが1580年8月4日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアキッドエルミンスターで生まれた時，父Sir John Popeは24歳，母Patience Alice Blakieは27歳であった。1610年以前にイギリスイギリスブリストルでBridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnothと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1649年6月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターで68歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Blake Pope II(이)가 1580년 8월 4일 영국 워스스터실르 키더민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Pope(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Patience Alice Blakie(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1610년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨에서 Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1649년 6월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Blake Pope II nasceu em 4 de agosto de 1580, em Kidderminster, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Pope, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Patience Alice Blakie, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth antes 1610, em Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de junho de 1649, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Coloni: массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Blake Pope II родился 4 августа 1580 г. в местности киддирминстир, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Pope было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Patience Alice Blakie, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth перед 1610 г. в местности бристол, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 1 июня 1649 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Blake Pope II nació el 4 de agosto de 1580, en Kidderminster, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Pope, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Patience Alice Blakie, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Bridget Marcy “Mary” Halsnoth antes de 1610, en Bristol, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 1 de junio de 1649, en Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
